title: Database DevOps
summary: Schema migrations with Flyway and Liquibase, zero-downtime migrations, expand-contract pattern, and DB CI/CD.

sections.content:
## Database Schema as Code

Database schema changes should be versioned, reviewed, and deployed with the same rigor as application code. Manual DDL scripts run on production are the source of many outages.

**Two leading tools:**
- **Flyway** — simple, SQL-based, convention over configuration
- **Liquibase** — XML/YAML/SQL changesets, rollback support, more features

sections.title: Flyway Migration Workflow
sections.content:
graph LR
    Dev[Developer writes\nV3__add_email_index.sql] --> Git[Git commit]
    Git --> CI[CI runs\nflyway validate]
    CI --> Deploy[Deploy app]
    Deploy --> Flyway[Flyway runs\non startup]
    Flyway --> Schema[Schema updated]
    Schema --> App[App starts]
    style Flyway fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style App fill:#059669,color:#fff

## Flyway Configuration

```sql
-- src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__create_orders_table.sql
CREATE TABLE orders (
    id          BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    customer_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL,
    status      VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    total       DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    created_at  TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- V2__add_order_reference.sql
ALTER TABLE orders ADD COLUMN reference VARCHAR(20);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_reference ON orders(reference);

-- R__create_order_summary_view.sql  (repeatable migration)
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW order_summary AS
SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count, SUM(total) as total
FROM orders GROUP BY status;
```

```yaml
# application.yml
spring:
  flyway:
    enabled: true
    locations: classpath:db/migration
    baseline-on-migrate: false  # fail if DB has tables without flyway history
    validate-on-migrate: true   # verify checksums match
    out-of-order: false         # reject migrations applied out of sequence
```

**Naming convention:**
- `V{version}__{description}.sql` — versioned (run once)
- `U{version}__{description}.sql` — undo migration (rollback)
- `R__{description}.sql` — repeatable (re-runs when checksum changes)

sections.title: Expand-Contract Migration Timeline
sections.content:
graph LR
    E[Expand\nAdd new column\nBackfill data] --> App2[Deploy new app\nUse new column]
    App2 --> C[Contract\nDrop old column]
    subgraph During Expand
        OldApp[Old app instances\nuse user_name]
        NewApp[New app instances\nuse full_name]
        OldApp & NewApp -->|both work| OK[No downtime]
    end
    style E fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style C fill:#059669,color:#fff
    style OK fill:#059669,color:#fff

sections.question: Why is the Expand-Contract pattern necessary for zero-downtime column renames?
options: Because databases don't support renaming columns directly
Because during a rolling deployment, old and new app versions run simultaneously — old instances need the old column, new instances need the new column
To prevent data loss during column type changes
Because Flyway doesn't support ALTER TABLE RENAME COLUMN
sections.explanation: During a rolling deployment, old and new app versions run simultaneously for minutes/hours. If you rename the column in a single migration, old instances crash (column not found). Expand-Contract adds the new column (both names exist), deploys new code (uses new name), then drops the old column only after all instances are updated — no downtime.
